player_utterance: You look like you've been holding something heavy for a long time.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, folding and unfolding the funeral program in his hands

player_utterance: We haven't. I'm just someone who wandered in out of the rain, honestly. But you looked like maybe you didn't want to be alone with whatever that program is turning into.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, program now still in one hand
sitting on the bench now, program flat on his knee

player_utterance: What did you know about him?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking at the program rather than at you
sitting on the bench, hands resting on his knees, looking out into the courtyard

voice_offers.read: Three questions into silence; pressing harder will close him.
The program itself is the artifact he keeps studying.
voice_offers.line: What does the program say about him? You keep looking at it.

player_utterance: You haven't said his name once.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, very still, hands no longer on the program

voice_offers.read: Silence is working; he is sitting with it now.
He's settling. One small question reopens what silence softened.
voice_offers.line: You haven't said his name once.

player_utterance: What does it cost you, to say it here — just to me, just tonight?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows finding his knees, hands loosely clasped

voice_offers.read: He flinched at the name's absence; press the vacuum.
voice_offers.line: Say it. Right now. Out loud.

voice_offers.read: His hands have stopped fidgeting; he is listening inward.
voice_offers.line: It's alright. You don't have to answer fast.

voice_offers.read: A teacher who won't name his subject has a reason.
voice_offers.line: You teach the Reformation. You name everyone. Why not him?

player_utterance: Whatever it costs, I'll sit with it. His name, and whatever comes after it — I'm not going anywhere.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking down at the program in his lap, very still

voice_offers.read: He's leaning in; the question landed. Hold the silence open.
voice_offers.line: His name. That's all I'm asking for.

voice_offers.read: The question is already doing the work; let him find it.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it costs — I'll sit with it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands clasped over the program, looking out into the courtyard

voice_offers.read: He's still; the offer is made; let it land.
He's gone quiet three times — one more push cracks it.
voice_offers.line: Daniel. That's his name, isn't it? You can say it after me.

player_utterance: You're already inside that passage. What would it mean to read it whole — out loud, to someone who's listening?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, unfolding the funeral program and smoothing it flat on his knee

voice_offers.read: Silence is working; his hands are clasped, he hasn't fled.
Two silences in a row is indulgence; the quiet needs a name in it.
voice_offers.line: You're quoting scripture in your head right now. Give me the name instead.

player_utterance: Pick one passage you've been carrying — and read it to me whole.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning forward, the program facedown on his knee, eyes on the candle

voice_offers.read: He's hiding inside text; make him exegete it aloud.
voice_offers.line: Read it from the program. Whatever verse you've been turning over — read it to me, the whole thing.

voice_offers.read: The open hand keeps winning and we're still circling.
voice_offers.line: You smoothed that program like it's his face. Whose funeral is this to you?

believer_state_after.posture: head bowed, elbows on knees, hands pressed together in front of his mouth

voice_offers.read: He is breaking; the third demand will shatter, not open.
voice_offers.line: ...you don't have to. I'm here either way.

voice_offers.read: He's already turned the program face down — push through the last inch.
voice_offers.line: Turn it over. Read it. You came here to say it out loud.

player_utterance: Take your time. The end of it has been waiting a long while already.

believer_state_after.posture: still bowed, hands open in his lap now, looking sometimes at the candle

voice_offers.read: He's bowed and silent; pressing now is cruelty, not courage.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. The passage will wait for you.

voice_offers.read: He's at the edge of saying it; one more nudge opens him.
voice_offers.line: Lazarus at the gate, or the rich man in torment — which one is you?

believer_state_after.posture: hands open in his lap, head still bowed, occasionally looking at the candle

voice_offers.read: He is bowed and open; pressure now would be cruelty.
Silence stretched twice already; one true question will land now.
voice_offers.line: Which verse, John. Say the words.

player_utterance: Whatever brought you to that door without it — it brought you here. That's enough.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting up slowly, finally — back against the bench, hands still open in his lap, looking at you for the first time in a while

voice_offers.read: He's already there; silence held him, presence holds him now.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it is — I'll sit with it when you're ready.

voice_offers.read: He's broken open; one true question lands now or never.
voice_offers.line: The verse, John. The one you've been carrying. Say it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting against the back of the bench, hands open in his lap, looking at you with wet eyes

voice_offers.read: He's looking up; meet the look, don't fill it.
voice_offers.line: There you are.

voice_offers.read: He's surfaced and ready; name the verse now.
voice_offers.line: Now. What was the verse.
